explicit_text: My great-grandmother, Karin Svenson Berg, was a remarkable woman who emigrated from Sweden in 1902. She was educated at the University of Lund and taught school in her hometown of Osby before coming to America. Though she was originally engaged to Andrew Berg, who sadly died before she arrived, she married his brother Nils after he invited her to come to Minnesota. Karin brought many cherished items with her, including gifts from her students and a basket box she carried on the boat, which have been passed down through our family.

explicit_text: Mora, Minnesota, was the first place my great-grandmother Karin Svenson Berg arrived at when she came to America. After traveling alone by steamship and train, she was met at the train depot in Mora by Nils Berg, who would become her husband. Mora was an important stop on her journey before they married at the Kanabec County Courthouse and moved on to live in Wahkon.
